title: Magical Mediterranean Japanese Cacao Mousse
description: A unique dessert combining Mediterranean and Japanese flavors with cacao, featuring a glassy-jelly texture achieved through spherification, served on a cedar plank.
instructions: Melt the dark chocolate chips in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, honey, and lemon juice until well combined.
In another bowl, mix the sodium alginate with a small amount of water to dissolve, then add it to the yogurt mixture and whisk until smooth.
Add the melted chocolate to the yogurt mixture and whisk until fully incorporated.
Prepare the calcium chloride bath by mixing it with 1 cup of water in a large bowl.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the chocolate mixture into the calcium chloride bath to create spheres.
Let the spheres sit in the bath for about 30 seconds to set, then remove them with a slotted spoon.
Place the spheres on the cedar plank, arranging them in a decorative pattern.
Refrigerate the dessert for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional honey or lemon zest if desired.
